pairs.question: When was Geffery Noblet born and when did he pass away?
pairs.answer: Geffery Noblet was born on 14 September 1916 and passed away on 16 August 2006.

pairs.question: How many Test matches did Geffery Noblet play for Australia, and in which years did he play them?
pairs.answer: Geffery Noblet played in three Test matches for Australia from 1950 to 1953.

pairs.question: What was Geffery Noblet's primary role in cricket, and for which domestic team did he play first-class cricket?
pairs.answer: Geffery Noblet was a fast-medium bowler who played first-class cricket for South Australia from 1945 to 1953.

pairs.question: What was Geffery Noblet's best bowling performance in a single domestic season for South Australia?
pairs.answer: In the 1948–49 Australian domestic season, Noblet took 38 wickets at an average of 15.4, which was the best performance by a South Australian bowler in 38 seasons. This remains the best bowling average for any South Australian bowler since Robert Rees took 10/129 in the 1909–10 season.

pairs.question: What unusual gift did Geffery Noblet request from the South Australian Cricket Association, and why was it notable?
pairs.answer: Geffery Noblet requested a set of coffee tables when he was presented with a gift by the South Australian Cricket Association in 1949–50. This was notable because his request was highly unconventional compared to typical cricketing gifts.

pairs.question: How did Geffery Noblet discover the correct spelling of his first name?
pairs.answer: Geffery Noblet was not aware of the legal spelling of his name until adulthood when he saw his birth certificate. His name was misspelled as 'Geffery' rather than 'Jeffery' by a family friend tasked with registering his birth.

pairs.question: What major honor was awarded to Geffery Noblet in 1995, and for what reason?
pairs.answer: In 1995, Geffery Noblet was awarded the Medal of the Order of Australia for his service to cricket.

pairs.question: What was Geffery Noblet's role during World War II, and in which division did he serve?
pairs.answer: During World War II, Geffery Noblet served in the transport division of the Australian Army.
